_doc: Single source of truth for generated calculator clusters. Edit here, then run `python build.py`. Pages marked custom:true are hand-written and left untouched by the generator.

car_loan.car_note: Car loan rates ≈ 5–7% p.a. Max tenure typically 5 years. Ijarah and Murabaha available at Al Baraka, Bahrain Islamic Bank, Al Salam.
car_loan.nuance: Islamic financing (Ijarah and Murabaha) is widely available alongside conventional loans.

car_loan.car_note: Car loan rates ≈ 4.5–6% p.a. (conventional). Shariah options (Murabaha/Ijarah) via QIB, Masraf Al Rayan. Max tenure 5–7 years depending on income.
car_loan.nuance: Shariah-compliant options (Murabaha and Ijarah) are common, and the maximum tenure depends on your income.

car_loan.car_note: Auto Murabaha effective rate ≈ 4–6% p.a. Max tenure 5 years. Providers: Al Rajhi Bank, SNB, Alinma, Bank Albilad. SIMAH credit check required.
car_loan.nuance: Most financing is Shariah-compliant Murabaha, and a SIMAH credit check is required.

car_loan.car_note: HP rates ≈ 7–10% APR, PCP rates ≈ 6–9% APR. PCP has a final balloon payment; HP pays off fully. Typical tenure 3–5 years. Dealers and banks both offer financing.
car_loan.nuance: Hire Purchase (HP) pays the car off in full, while PCP has a lower monthly cost but a final balloon payment.

car_loan.car_note: Secured car loans ≈ 6.5–9% p.a., unsecured 10–14%. Max tenure 7 years. Dealer finance sometimes has balloon payment options. Providers: CommBank, Westpac, Toyota Finance, Plenti, Pepper Money.
car_loan.nuance: Secured loans (where the car is collateral) are cheaper than unsecured personal loans, and some dealer finance includes a balloon payment due at the end.

car_loan.car_note: Car loan rates ≈ 11–14% p.a. Max tenure 6 years for new cars. DBBL, BRAC, Eastern Bank offer auto loans.
car_loan.nuance: Both new and used vehicles can be financed, with used cars usually carrying a slightly higher rate.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loan rates ≈ 1.8–2.5% a.m. (22–30% p.a. effective). Down payment typical 20–30%. Max tenure 5 years (60 months). Providers: Bradesco Financiamentos, Itaú, Santander Financiamentos, BV, Omni.
car_loan.nuance: Rates are usually quoted per month and are among the highest of any major market, so the effective annual cost is steep.

car_loan.car_note: New car loans ≈ 6–8% p.a., used ≈ 8–11%. Tenure up to 96 months (8 years). Manufacturer promos ≈ 0–3.99% on select models. Providers: TD Auto Finance, Scotiabank, RBC, Ford Credit Canada.
car_loan.nuance: Manufacturers often run promotional rates around 0–3.99% on select models.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loan rates ≈ 4–7% p.a. Min 20% down for conventional, 15% for new-energy vehicles (NEVs). Max tenure 5 years. Providers: ICBC, BoC, BYD Auto Finance, Geely Auto Finance, Toyota Auto Finance.
car_loan.nuance: New-energy vehicles (NEVs) qualify for a lower minimum down payment.

car_loan.car_note: Car loan rates ≈ 22–26% p.a. New and used financing available. Min 20% down payment typical. Providers: CIB, QNB Alahli, NBE, Banque Misr.
car_loan.nuance: Both new and used cars can be financed.

car_loan.car_note: Car loan rates ≈ 5–7% p.a. Manufacturer financing (BMW, VW, Mercedes) often has promotional 0% deals on specific models. Typical tenure 3–7 years.
car_loan.nuance: Manufacturer financing often includes promotional 0% deals on specific models.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loan rates ≈ 4–6% p.a. Lenders: HSBC, Hang Seng, Bank of East Asia, plus dealer-arranged finance. First registration tax is steep, making cars expensive.
car_loan.nuance: First-registration tax is steep, which raises the total cost of buying a car; dealer-arranged finance is also common.

car_loan.car_note: Car loan rates ≈ 1.9–4.5% p.a. (dealer financing) or 1.5–3% (bank loans). Max tenure 7 years. Providers: Toyota Financial Services, Nissan Financial, Rakuten Bank, Orix Auto Leasing.
car_loan.nuance: Bank loans are usually cheaper than dealer financing.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loans ≈ 13–18% p.a. 20–30% down payment typical. Tenure up to 6 years. Providers: KCB, NCBA, Stanbic, Co-op Bank, Absa.
car_loan.nuance: Both new and used vehicles can be financed, with used cars usually carrying a slightly higher rate.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loan rates ≈ 4.5–6% p.a. (conventional), capped at CBK discount + 3%. Max 5 years. Islamic (Murabaha) via KFH, Boubyan, Warba.
car_loan.nuance: Conventional rates are capped at the CBK discount rate + 3%, and Islamic Murabaha is widely available.

car_loan.car_note: HP flat rate ≈ 2–3.5% p.a. (reducing balance ≈ 4–7%). Max 9 years new, 7 years used. Providers: Maybank, CIMB, Public Bank. Islamic AITAB via Bank Islam.
car_loan.nuance: Rates are usually quoted as a flat rate, and Islamic AITAB financing is available via Bank Islam.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loans ≈ 11–16% p.a. Min 10–20% down. Max tenure 60-72 months. Captive financing (Ford Credit, Toyota Financial México) often competitive. Providers: BBVA, HSBC, Banorte, Ford Credit.
car_loan.nuance: Captive financing (Ford Credit, Toyota Financial México) is often competitive.

car_loan.car_note: Car loan rates ≈ 8–14% p.a. Main lenders: MTF, UDC, Heartland, Motor Trade Finances, plus bank-owned (ASB, ANZ). Peer-to-peer options via Harmoney.
car_loan.nuance: Peer-to-peer finance (for example Harmoney) is also an option.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loan rates ≈ 22–28% p.a. State auto-subsidy program offers reduced rates for domestic vehicles. Max tenure 5 years. Providers: Sberbank, Gazprombank, RSHB, Rosbank, VTB.
car_loan.nuance: A state auto-subsidy programme offers reduced rates on qualifying domestic vehicles.

car_loan.car_note: Car loan rates ≈ 2.5–4% p.a. flat (roughly 4.5–7.5% reducing equivalent). MAS rules: Max 60% LTV for cars ≤S$20k OMV, 70% for higher. Max tenure 7 years. Providers: DBS, UOB, Hong Leong Finance.
car_loan.nuance: MAS rules cap the loan at 60% of the car's value (OMV up to S$20k) or 70% above that.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loan rates ≈ 4.5–7% p.a. Max 5–7 years. Captives: Hyundai Capital, KB Capital. Banks: KB, Shinhan, Hana, plus card companies (Shinhan Card, Samsung Card).
car_loan.nuance: Captive lenders and card companies (Shinhan Card, Samsung Card) also finance cars.

car_loan.car_note: Vehicle loan rates ≈ 12–14% p.a. Post-cyclone vehicle imports surged 2025. Max tenure typically 7 years. Providers: Commercial Bank, DFCC, HNB, Seylan.
car_loan.nuance: Vehicle imports surged after import restrictions eased in 2025.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loan rates ≈ 3.5–5% p.a. Tenure up to 84 months. Major lenders: CTBC, Taishin, E.Sun, Fubon, plus captives (Hotai, Toyota Leasing).
car_loan.nuance: Captive lenders (Hotai, Toyota Leasing) are common for new cars.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loan flat rates ≈ 2.5–4.5% (effective ~5–9% reducing). Max tenure 84 months. Providers: Toyota Leasing, Siam Kubota, bank auto loans (KBank, SCB, Krungthai).
car_loan.nuance: Rates are usually quoted as a flat rate, so the effective reducing-balance cost is higher.

car_loan.car_note: New car loans ≈ 7–8% p.a., used car ≈ 9–11%. Captive lenders (Toyota Financial, Ford Credit) and banks (Chase, Capital One) are common. Credit score materially affects rate.
car_loan.nuance: Your credit score materially affects the rate you are offered.

car_loan.car_note: Auto loan rates ≈ 9–12% p.a. Tenure up to 7–8 years. Major lenders: Vietcombank, Techcombank, VPBank (FE Credit), plus Toyota Financial Services.
car_loan.nuance: Both new and used vehicles can be financed.

sip.invest_note: ASX 200 long-term ≈ 8–9% (incl. dividends). Super contribution cap A$30k/yr concessional. Platforms: CommSec, SelfWealth, Vanguard Personal Investor, Stake.
Bahrain All-Share Index long-term ≈ 5–7% BHD. Asset managers: Investcorp, SICO, NBB Capital. Bahrain is a regional Islamic finance hub.
Ibovespa long-term ≈ 12–15% nominal. Tesouro Selic ≈ Selic rate (15%). Platforms: XP, Clear, Rico, Nubank Invest, Inter Invest. Tax: 15–22.5% on gains (progressive).
TFSA limit C$7k/yr (2026), RRSP up to 18% of income. TSX long-term ≈ 7–9%. S&P 500 via VFV or XUS. Platforms: Wealthsimple, Questrade, TD Direct Investing.
CSI 300 long-term ≈ 6–9%. Platforms: Ant Fortune, Tiantian Fund, E Fund. Capital controls limit foreign investing; QDII funds provide limited access.
EGX 30 long-term ≈ 18–22% nominal (EGP). Inflation high, so consider USD-linked or gold certificates. Providers: EFG Hermes, CI Capital.
MSCI Europe long-term ≈ 7–8% p.a. ETF providers: iShares, Amundi, Xtrackers.
Hang Seng Index long-term ≈ 5–8% HKD. Platforms: HSBC, Hang Seng Invest, Futu, Tiger Brokers, Interactive Brokers. MPF employer+employee 5%+5% mandatory.
Nikkei 225 long-term ≈ 5–7% nominal, global ETFs ≈ 7–10% in JPY terms. New NISA annual limit ¥3.6M (¥1.8M accumulation). Platforms: SBI Securities, Rakuten Securities, Monex.
MMFs ≈ 9–13% p.a. (Cytonn, CIC, Sanlam). NSE long-term ≈ 10–13%. M-Shwari/KCB M-Pesa for short-term savings.
Boursa Kuwait Premier long-term ≈ 6–8% KWD. Major asset managers: NBK Capital, KFH Capital, KAMCO, Markaz. Retirement: PIFSS for nationals, private plans for expats.
ASB long-term ≈ 5–6% tax-free. KLCI long-term ≈ 7–8%. EPF contributions also key for retirement.
IPC long-term ≈ 9–12% nominal. Afores: voluntary contributions on top of mandatory 6.5%. Platforms: GBM+, Actinver, Kuspit, Fintual, Bitso (crypto).
KiwiSaver growth funds long-term ≈ 7–8% NZD. Default providers: ANZ, ASB, BNZ, Westpac, Kiwi Wealth. Plus Simplicity, Milford, Generate, Sharesies.
NGX All-Share long-term ≈ 15–20% (nominal, NGN). Many investors use USD funds via Cowrywise, Bamboo to hedge inflation.
QE Index long-term ≈ 5–7% QAR. Many expats invest via brokers in UAE (EFG Hermes, ADCB Securities). QFC-licensed platforms also available.
MOEX long-term ≈ 15–20% nominal. OFZ (government bonds) yields ≈ 15%. Many Russians hold gold or foreign-currency deposits. Platforms: Tinkoff Investments, Sber Invest, Finam.
Tadawul TASI long-term ≈ 9–11%. Shariah-screened indexes popular. Al Rajhi Capital, SNB Capital are major asset managers.
STI long-term ≈ 5–7%. Most Singaporeans use global ETFs (VWRA, CSPX) via Endowus, SYFE, Interactive Brokers. CPF SA earns 4%/yr guaranteed.
TFSA limit R36k/year, R500k lifetime. JSE Top 40 long-term ≈ 10–12%. Providers: EasyEquities, Satrix, Allan Gray, Coronation.
KOSPI long-term ≈ 5–8% KRW. Major AMCs: Samsung, Mirae Asset, KB, Shinhan. Retail platforms: Toss Invest, Kakao Pay Securities. IRP gets tax deduction up to 9M KRW/yr.
TAIEX long-term ≈ 7–9% TWD. ETF leaders: Yuanta (00878, 0050), Cathay (00929), Fubon. Many retail investors use SIPs into dividend ETFs.
SET Index long-term ≈ 6–9% THB. Major AMCs: KAsset, SCBAM, BBLAM, UOBAM, TISCO. Retirement (RMF) and long-term (SSF) funds are tax-deductible.
BIST 100 nominal ≈ 35–45% p.a. (but TRY inflation is ~35%, so real returns are modest). Many Turks use gold, USD, or Eurobond funds to hedge. Platforms: Garanti BBVA, İş Yatırım.
ADX and DFM long-term ≈ 8–10%. International ETFs via Interactive Brokers, Sarwa, StashAway popular for expats.
ISA allowance £20k/year tax-free. Providers: Vanguard, Hargreaves Lansdown, AJ Bell. FTSE 100 historical ≈ 7%.
Vanguard VOO/VTI, Fidelity FZROX. S&P 500 historical average ≈ 10% nominal. Use 401(k), Roth IRA for tax advantages.
VN-Index long-term ≈ 10–14% VND (high volatility). Fund managers: Dragon Capital, VinaCapital, Techcom Capital, SSI Asset Management. Retail platforms: Finhay, Fincorp, Tikop.

fixed_deposit.fd_note: Term deposit rates ≈ 4.2–4.8% p.a. for 1-2 year terms. Top rates at Macquarie, Judo Bank, Bank of Sydney. Compare on Canstar, Mozo.
Term deposit rates ≈ 3.5–4.5% p.a. Top yields at NBB, BBK, Ahli United for 1+ year tenures.
FDR rates ≈ 9–11% p.a. for 3+ year tenures. Islamic banks offer Mudarabah-based alternatives with similar profit rates.
CDBs ≈ 100–120% of CDI (≈ 13–16% p.a.). LCI/LCA tax-free on real estate/agro. Tesouro Selic ≈ Selic. Platforms: all major broker apps.
GIC rates ≈ 3.5–4.25% p.a. for 1-5 year terms. Top rates at EQ Bank, Oaken, Tangerine. Tax-free inside a TFSA.
Time deposit rates ≈ 1.5–2.5% p.a. for 1-5 year terms. Large state banks lower; small/regional banks slightly higher. Rates have fallen significantly over the past 3 years.
Gold certificates and standard savings certs ≈ 18–23% p.a. (yields dropped after 2024–2026 rate cuts). 3-year tenure most common. Issued by NBE, Banque Misr.
Fixed deposit rates ≈ 2.5–3.5% p.a. Higher rates in Central/Eastern Europe. Check Raisin or Scalable for comparison.
HKD time deposit ≈ 2.5–3.5% for 1-yr. USD deposits often higher. Top rates at virtual banks: ZA Bank, Mox, livi, WeLab.
Bank FDs: SBI, HDFC, ICICI typically 6–7% p.a. for 5+ year tenures. Senior citizens get +0.5%. Interest is taxable as per slab.
Deposito rates ≈ 4.5–5.5% p.a. at major banks. LPS guarantee rate (Oct 2025): 4.00% for conventional, 6.25% for rural banks.
Time deposit rates ≈ 0.3–0.6% p.a. for 1-5 year terms. Rates edging up from historic zero-lower-bound. Top rates at online banks: SBI Shinsei, Aeon Bank.
FD rates ≈ 7–12% p.a. depending on bank. Treasury bills (91/182/364 day) often pay similar or better.
FD rates ≈ 3–4% p.a. Islamic profit-sharing (Mudarabah) similar. Top rates typically at NBK, Gulf Bank, KIB.
FD rates ≈ 3.5–4% p.a. for 12+ months. PIDM insured up to RM250k. Islamic Commodity Murabaha deposits similar rates.
Pagaré rates ≈ 8–10% p.a. for 1-yr tenures. CETES (28/91/182/364d) via Cetesdirecto ≈ 9%. Top bank rates at Banco Azteca, Banco del Bajío.
Term deposit rates ≈ 3.5–4.5% for 1-yr. No deposit guarantee scheme yet (one planned). Top rates often at smaller banks: SBS, Heartland, Rabobank.
Term deposit rates ≈ 12–18% p.a. Treasury bills often comparable. NDIC insures deposits up to ₦500K.
Regular Income Certificates ≈ 13% p.a., Behbood Savings ≈ 16.5% (seniors/widows), Defense Savings Certificates 10-yr ≈ 13%. Bank term deposits ≈ 11–12%.
Time deposit rates ≈ 4.5–5.5% p.a. for 1+ year terms. Digital banks (CIMB, Maya Bank, ING) offer 4–6% on savings.
Term deposit rates ≈ 3.5–4.5% p.a. QNB, CBQ, Doha Bank offer higher tiers for larger deposits. Islamic profit-sharing alternatives at QIB, Masraf Al Rayan.
Deposit rates ≈ 14–18% p.a. for 6-12 month tenures. Short-term rates near CBR key rate. Top banks: Sberbank, VTB, Alfa, Tinkoff.
Mudarabah term deposits ≈ 4–5% expected profit rate. Al Rajhi, Alinma are major Islamic banks.
FD rates ≈ 2.5–3% p.a. for 1-year tenures. T-bills (6-month) often better yield. Compare at DBS, UOB, OCBC, ICBC SG, Maybank.
FD rates ≈ 8–9% p.a. for 12+ month terms. Tax-free up to R23,800/year interest exemption (under 65). Providers: African Bank, Capitec, FNB.
1-yr FD rates ≈ 2.8–3.3%. Internet banks (Kakao, Toss, K Bank) often beat traditional banks. Savings banks (저축은행) pay higher but watch KDIC limit.
FD rates ≈ 8–10% p.a. for 1+ year. Senior citizens +1%. Licensed finance companies offer higher rates but have higher risk.
1-yr TD rates ≈ 1.5–1.8% p.a. Foreign currency TDs (USD, AUD) can pay much higher — popular among savers.
FD rates ≈ 1.25–2% for 1-yr. Higher at smaller banks (KTB, TMB, CIMB Thai). Digital accounts from Kbank (K-Plus), SCB (EASY) sometimes beat this.
Vadeli mevduat ≈ 38–42% p.a. for 3-month tenures. Most deposits are short-term due to rate volatility. Withholding tax 7.5–15% on interest.
FD rates ≈ 3.5–4.5% p.a. Islamic alternatives: Wakalah/Mudarabah deposits at similar profit rates.
Fixed-rate bonds ≈ 4–4.5% p.a. for 1–3 year terms. Best rates on MoneySavingExpert or Raisin UK.
CD rates ≈ 4–5% p.a. for 1–5 year terms. Top providers: Marcus by Goldman, Ally Bank, Capital One 360.
FD rates ≈ 5–7% p.a. for 1-yr. Higher at private banks (VP, VIB, NCB) vs state-owned (VCB, BIDV). Gold-linked savings also popular.
